notes: 5 January: Beheading of 5 citizens in Douaouda (Tipaza).
10 February: 25 people killed in Hammam Melouane (Blida) by an armed group.
22 July: 11 people killed in Benachour (Blida) by an armed group.
12 January: 14 citizens were murdered in Tabannant (Bouinan, Blida) by an armed group. The victims had sons who joined the underground resistance according to testimonies collected.
On 3 August 1997, 19 young citizens were killed in Amroussa (Bouinan, Blida) by an armed group. It was discovered that the victims were citizens who had belonged to the FIS.
1 February: 7 citizens of a farming estate (Haouch Louz) located in LarbaG (Blida) werekilled by an armed group.
22 April: 42 people killed in El Omaria (MTdTa).
24 March: 5 young girls were killed in Berrouaghia by an armed group.
25 July: 13 citizens of the Sidi-Salem doyar near Omaria (MTdTa) were killed by an armed group.

16 February: killing of two families from the El Karrech doyar (Blida). The residents of this doyar had refused to set up militias. One of these families was burnt alive in its home after the armed group set fire to it: 33 people perished.
A policeman was killed in Algiers.
12 August: Killing of 29 citizens in the Hraouat doyar at Ain Defla by an armed group.

14 July: An armed group introducing itself as mujahedeen entered the village of Tafraout(Ain Boucif. MTdTa) to demand some food. After having been well fed and cared for, they turned tothe villagers and kill twelve.
30 July: Killing of 38 citizens in the village of Sidi-Madani (Blida) by an armed group.Killing of 22 citizens in LarbaG (Blida) by an armed group.
The vice-mayor of Bachdjarah was killed near his residence.
14 June: 16 citizens killed in Haouch Sahraoui (Blida) by an armed group.

20 August: Over 60 citizens including women and children were killed in the Souhanedoyar near Tablat (MTdTa) according to information from survivors who were wounded by bulletsand hospitalised in the Zemirli d'El Harrach hospital. Witnesses said t
24 June: Assassination of 15 citizen in Sanda by an armed group.
A policeman and 4 members of his family were assassinated in Baraki.
Explosion of a bomb in Ain Fakroun (Oum Bouaghi). 7 dead and 11 injured.
Jan 23: The Sifouane, Benmahdi and Loukal families living in Haouch El Hadjin Baba Ali (close to Algiers) were decimated by an armed group. A total of 15 people including 9women were murdered.
16 June: 48 citizens killed in the village of Danat Labguer (M'sila) by an armed group.

11 April: 23 citizens including 12 women and 6 children were killed in the Menaa village (Boufarik). This killing took place only a few hours after the death of 5 militia men in a bomb explosion in Haouch Gros (Boufarik).
26 May: a family of 8 people was assassinated by an armed group in the village of Djebabra(MTdTa). Thirteen citizen were assassinated in Douaouda (Tipaza) by an armed group.
5 April: 12 citizens including 7 women were killed by an armed group in Bouinan (Blida).
5 August: 9 citizens killed in the Benamor area of Oued Slama (Blida) by armed men.
26 August: Killing of 64 citizens including around 30 women in the BTni Ali doyar near ChrTa (Blida) by an armed group.
24 January: 35 citizens living in the Ouled Ali doyar (Berrouaghia) were murdered by animportant armed group.
19 August: 20 citizens were killed in Fand El Batma (Djelfa) by an armed group.
31 August: 19 citizens, members of two families, were killed in Bologhine, in the middle of the capital, by armed men. 32 citizens were killed in the region of Tissemsilt by an important armed group.
30 July: Killing of 22 citizens in LarbaG (Blida) by an armed group.
29 July: Killing of 39 citizens, including women and children, in the Matmata doyar (AnnDefla) by an armed group.
5 January: Killing of 18 citizens in the Oliviers district of Douaouda (Tipaza). Among the victims are 3 children and 6 women. This region had refused to organise armed militias.
6 militants were killed during a military operation in Draa, near Mostaganem.
11 July: 14 people were killed in the Balili doyar (Bou Ismanl-Tipaza) by an armed group.
7 January: Explosion of a bomb in the Didouche Mourad street in Algiers: 20 dead.
Explosion of a bomb near a market in Laarba (Blibda). 1 dead and 6 injured.
4 armed men were killed by security forces in Ain Benian. The armed men have been responsible for an earlier assassination of 4 inhabitants of the city.
21 July: Killing of 21 citizens in the M'ghita doyar, in ChrTa (Blida) by an armed group.
31 July: 20 people killed by an armed group in a doyar located near the roman ruins of Tipaza.
8 August: 11 people killed in Medghoussa (Tiaret) by an armed group.
13 April: 32 citizens were killed by an armed group in the Chaib farm in Chebli (Blida).
23 March: 32 people, including 14 women, were mutilated and killed in Ouled Antar (Ksar Boukhari).
7 August: 21 people were killed in the village of Zaboudja (MTdTa).
24 July: Over 30 citizens of the Sidi-Ghiat and Oued Bouhradoun doyars (Hadjout Tipaza) Including children and women were murdered by an armed group.
6 April: 15 people killed by an armed group in Ann Lahdid (Tiaret). 13 people killedin the Merniche doyar (Tlemcen) by an armed group. 52 people killed in the village of Thalit(MTdTa) by armed men.
27 June: 22 people were killed in the Zmala doyar, Seghouane village (MTdTa) by an armed group.
14 May: 29 people including around ten children and women were killed after having beenawfully mutilated in the Faner farm (Chebli. Blida) by an armed group.
2 June: Explosion of a bomb in the working-class area of the Casbah in Algiers: 10 dead.

Explosion of a car bomb on the Boulevard des Martyrs in Algiers, near the military hospital of Ain Nadjaa. 17 dead and several injured. Another car bomb exploded near the parking 'Riad el Feth' in Algiers. 2 dead and several injured.
A chauffeur from the governmental newspaper El Moudjahid was killed by armed men in Bachdjarah (Algiers).
6 January: 23 citizens were horribly mutilated and killed in Hadjout (Tipaza) by an armed group.
22 February: 6 family members living in Tablat (MTdTa) were murdered by an armed group.
Jan 22: 23 people killed in El Omaria (MTdTa).

21 April: An armed group on horses (!) raided a farm (Haouch Boughelaf) located at theentrance of Bougara (Blida) and proceeded to the horrible killing of 120 people (includingdozens of women and children). It is of note that a military camp, the mili
29 August: Bomb explosion in the working-class area of the Casbah in Algiers: 13 killed andover 60 wounded.
8 civilians, among them one baby, were assassinated on a farm in Sidi Kaddour, situated in Sidi Moussa (Alger).
3 July: 18 citizens were murdered in Ouzra, a village close to MTdTa by an armed group.
24 August: 29 citizens were killed in the Omaria doyar (MTdTa) by an armed group.
17 January: 43 citizens were murdered in Sidi-Abdelaziz in the region of BTni Slimane(MTdTa) and 6 others in the village of Bouchrahil, in the same region.
A retired general was killed near his residence in Saint Hubert in Oran.
A peasant and his son (12 years) were killed on a farm in Douaouda (Tipaza).

28 August: Killing in the area of Er-Rais located near Sidi-Moussa (Algiers). Heavily armed men came in a truck convoy at 11:45 PM a few hundred meters away from a military camp and butchered the population. More than 300 citizens were killed and 200 others wounded. Some of the armed men were wearing masks and others fake beards, according to many concurring survivors testimonies. They had Kalashnikovs on which electric torches were fixed, 'commando' daggers and axes.
A student of law-studies was killed by armed men in Sidi Moussa.
Explosion of a bomb near a market in Boufarik. 15 dead and 43 injured.
22 citizens were killed in Omaria (MTdTa) by an armed group.
4 February: 28 people killed in Boumedfaa (Ain Defla).
Two citizens were beheaded in Hassasna.
The president of the chamber of bailiffs of the Jijel court was killed by armed men.
Explosion of a car bomb in the village of 'Qautre Chemins' between Boufarik and Douera. 9 dead and 30 injured according to hospital sources.

19 January: Explosion of a car bomb in the main street of Algiers working-class district ofBelouizdad, one hour after the fast was broken. A true carnage: 54 citizens were killed and 89others wounded, according to the count from different hospitals
Explosion of a bomb near to the Lycee Ourida Meddad d'el Harrach. No victims

13 August: Killing in the Ouled Djillali douar, near DouTra (Algiers) perpetrated by an armed group against families who had fled the killings in Jijel and MTdTa. Toll: 15 dead including women and children.
4 militants were killed during a military operation in Chlef.
23 January: Explosion of a bomb in the middle of a market in the working-class area ofBaraki (Algiers): 30 dead.
12 July: 33 citizens were killed by an armed group in the Fetha doyar close to Ksar Boukhari(MTdTa).
26 May: a family of 8 people was assassinated by an armed group in the village of Djebabra(MTdTa). Thirteen citizen were assassinated in Douaouda (Tipaza) by an armed group.
4 militants were killed during a military operation in Chlef.
3 February: 9 people of the same family were killed in horrible conditions in Benchicao(MTdTa).
23 August: 24 citizens from the town of BTthia (Ain Defla) were killed by an armed group.
6 July: 27 people were killed by an armed group in Ann Boucif (MTdTa).
22 January: 14 people were murdered in the village of Benramdane in Saoula (Tipaza).
11 January: 5 citizens killed in Ouled Chebel by an armed group.
Explosion of a bomb near a coffeehouse in Oued el Alleug (Blida). 4 dead and more than 15 injured.
29 May: 14 people killed in Bakoura, near Cherchell, by armed men.
April 3:In the village of Thalit (Ksar Boukhari), an armed group killed more than 50 citizens

27 July: 36 citizens were killed in the district of Si Zerrouk at the entrance of the city of Larbaa (Blida), located less than a hundred meters away from military barracks and a gendarmerie brigade. Electricity had been cut in the afternoon, according to survivors testimonies. The armed group used bombs to blow up houses.
2 July: 4 people of the same family were killed and six girls kidnapped in LarbaG (Blida) by agroup of around 30 armed men.

4 January: 16 citizens were murdered in the village of Benachour (Blida) by masked men a few hundred meters away from a military camp. It is of note that the citizens of this village had refused to set up armed militias.

18 July: 14 people killed in Bou Ismail by armed men.
A police inspector was killed in the Savignon neighbourhood of Oran by armed men.
3 April: 13 people were killed by an armed group in the village of Ann El Hadid (Tiaret).
11 April: 7 people killed in la Chiffa (Blida) by an armed group.
Two citizens were killed in a raid on the village of Hassi El Abd. Several televisions were destroyed and the school of the village burned.
Clashes between the military and militants in the region of Douade (Tipaza).

31 January: The area of Ktiten located in the region of Medea was attacked by dozens of armed masked men. 35 people including women and children and mostly relatives of Ali Benhadjar, chief of a local group which had broken ties with the GIA, were murdered and some horribly mutilated. The citizens of this region had refused to set up armed militias.
25 April: Bomb explosion in a train at the level of Baba Ali (Blida): 21 dead.
Explosion of a bomb near a market in Blida. 15 dead and around 30 injured.
Clashes between the military and militants in the 'Basse Casbah' for the whole night. No casualty figures given.
4 April: In Amroussa (Chebli. Blida), two families composed of 15 members, including sevenwomen and three children, were exterminated by an armed group.
11 June: 12 people were killed in Cheranfia, near Boufarik, by armed individuals.

actor1: PAGAD: People Against Gangsterism and Drugs
notes: A small army of about 30 heavily armed Pagad members invaded the Bo-Kaap early today, opening fire on the home of an alleged drug dealer with pistols and shotguns before setting fire to it.
A prisoner is killed and several others wounded when police stormed their cells after they took hostages and made demands to the warden at St. Albans prison outside of Port Elizabeth.
Six are dead and hundreds hurt when two rival political party militias in an historically Zulu town renew their violent activities.
Zulu nationals protest to commemorate the death of their brethren on the third anniversary of their death. Police intervene.

assoc_actor_1: NEHAWU: National Education, Health and Allied Workers Union
notes: Public sector workers took to the streets in a countrywide demonstration in support of government wage demands.
Around 50,000 employees of banks stage a street protest of the increasing violence they have experienced due to street gangs and corruption.

At least four pro-Mpumalanga demonstrators, including an ANC councillor, were shot and injured by police after one of their patrol vehicles was burnt by rioting residents in Bushbuckridge on Thursday afternoon.
Demonstrators gather during a speech by President Mandela to protest what they see as a lack of AIDS research by the government.
Elsewhere in the greater Bushbuckridge region, thousands of residents marched on the police station in Acornhoek.
Pro-Mpumalanga protesters march in the streets in towns adjacent to Groblersdal.

A group of demonstrators blocked roadways in protest of the threat of civic services to be cut due to non-payment and increasing rent. Police disperse the crowd with tear gas and rubber bullets. A seven year-old-boy is trampled to death amid the chaos, and others die
Four people were killed on Wednesday in communal violence between rival Zulu factions in South Africas troubled eastern KwaZulu-Natal province.
A group of robbers steal 50 guns from a police station.
COSATU members protest outside the Swaziland High Commission in Pretoria, demanding the release of arrest trade union members taken during a recent protest for democracy in that country.
Police had to fire tear-gas to disperse hundreds of students who attended a student council meeting at the University of KwaZulu Natal on Monday.

actor1: PAGAD: People Against Gangsterism and Drugs
notes: In Athlone a car was damaged early yesterday after a home-made device exploded on it.

On Monday morning, unknown assailants hurled a smoke grenade at a home in Mitchells Plain, causing minor damage; Several minutes later, meanwhile, another house in the same neighborhood was raked by gunfire, injuring 23-year-old Anthony Joseph in the arm and stomach. It is believed that the attack is part of ongoing clashes between gangsters and Muslim vigilante group PAGAD.
Two Zulu nationals were killed ahead of ceremony to commemorate the deaths of 50 three years ago. It is suspects that this killing was by the police.
The Boere Attack Troop, a far right group, detonates three explosives near a Mosque, post office, and a local shop. Extensive property damage, three children and an adult killed.
